overview.title: Centralized Market Data Store Implementation
overview.problem: NeuroSense FX lacks a single source of truth for market data. Each component (FloatingDisplay, PriceTicker, FxBasketDisplay) maintains its own lastData ref, leading to potential data inconsistencies, duplicated subscription logic, and no latency visibility for trading decisions.
overview.approach: Incremental phase-based migration to a centralized marketDataStore.js following the established workspace.js pattern. Create per-symbol writable stores with centralized subscription management, latency instrumentation, and schema versioning. Migrate components one at a time with rollback capability.

decisions.decision: Incremental phase-based migration with per-phase rollback capability
decisions.reasoning_chain: Big-bang migration risks system-wide failure -> Per-component rollback allows safe recovery without affecting other components -> Feature flags would add dual-maintenance overhead -> Incremental migration with rollback is the safest approach for a production trading system

decisions.decision: Per-symbol writable stores with centralized subscription manager
decisions.reasoning_chain: Single giant store creates monolithic state with poor encapsulation -> Component-scoped stores duplicate subscriptions and miss deduplication -> Per-symbol stores allow independent lifecycle AND centralized subscriptions enable deduplication -> Best of both approaches for a multi-symbol trading system

decisions.decision: Integrate FX Basket state machine into marketDataStore
decisions.reasoning_chain: State machine needs access to all 30 symbol stores -> Keeping separate requires circular imports or prop drilling -> Integration provides single source of truth with proper encapsulation while maintaining state machine logic

decisions.decision: Store follows workspace.js pattern with getState() method
decisions.reasoning_chain: workspace.js pattern is established in the codebase -> Consistent API reduces learning curve for developers -> getState() enables non-reactive access matching priceMarkerInteraction pattern requirements

decisions.decision: Dev-mode only validation preserves zero production overhead constraint
decisions.reasoning_chain: dataContracts.js:335 explicitly requires zero production overhead -> import.meta.env.DEV guards are already established pattern -> Preserves existing validation pattern without runtime cost in production

decisions.decision: Schema version 1.0.0 added to all WebSocket messages
decisions.reasoning_chain: No versioning currently exists in WebSocket protocol -> Breaking changes could silently corrupt data without detection -> Version field enables future compatibility checks and incremental rollout with fallback support

decisions.decision: Symbol change handling preserves reactive cleanup pattern
decisions.reasoning_chain: Existing components use reactive blocks for symbol changes -> Store subscription must integrate with Svelte reactive lifecycle -> Reactive cleanup with previousSymbol tracking ensures no stale data or subscription leaks

rejected_alternatives.rejection_reason: Current architecture is acceptable for visualization purposes. Incremental improvement preferred over risky full rewrite.
DataRouter is intentionally designed for parallel feeds without aggregation. This is a feature, not a bug.
Dual maintenance overhead during transition period. Per-phase rollback provides safety without code duplication.

rejected_alternatives.alternative: Single global store for all symbols
rejected_alternatives.rejection_reason: Creates monolithic state with poor encapsulation. Per-symbol stores allow independent lifecycle management.

constraints.description: Maintain backward compatibility with existing WebSocket message format
Keep DataRouter pattern of parallel feeds without aggregation
All components use unified data handling pattern after migration
No latency tracking in hot path that impacts performance
Svelte derived stores must work with existing reactive patterns

risks.risk: Symbol change reactive blocks may not clean up subscriptions properly
risks.mitigation: Use explicit previousSymbol tracking and unsubscribe in reactive block before new subscription

risks.risk: FX Basket 30-symbol subscription may overwhelm connection on startup
risks.mitigation: Preserve existing 600ms rate limiting between subscriptions

risks.risk: Store reference changes when symbol changes may cause Svelte reactivity issues
risks.mitigation: Use reactive assignment pattern: $: marketData = getMarketDataStore(formattedSymbol); $: lastData = $marketData;

risks.risk: Backend latency timestamps may not be present in all messages
risks.mitigation: Make latency fields optional and handle missing timestamps gracefully

invisible_knowledge.system: Callback-based reactivity with no central store. Each component maintains own lastData ref. WebSocket -> subscriptionManager.dispatch() -> component callback -> Svelte $: reactivity. DataRouter intentionally designed for parallel feeds without aggregation - this is a feature not a bug.
invariants: WebSocket message format must remain stable for backward compatibility
Schema version field is optional for v1.0.0 - frontend handles missing version gracefully
Dev-mode only validation via import.meta.env.DEV guards - zero production overhead
Symbol:source subscription keys (e.g., EURUSD:ctrader) enable multi-source but prevent aggregation
DataRouter parallel feeds architecture must be preserved - no backend aggregation
tradeoffs: Per-symbol stores chosen over single global store for better encapsulation at cost of slight complexity
Dev-mode validation chosen over production validation to satisfy zero overhead constraint
Incremental migration chosen over feature flags to avoid dual-maintenance overhead
Integrated FX Basket state machine chosen over separate module to avoid circular imports

architecture.diagram_ascii:
Data Sources                    Frontend
-------------                   --------
cTrader API ──┐                 ┌─ FloatingDisplay.svelte
              ├── DataRouter ───┼─ PriceTicker.svelte
TradingView ──┘   (backend)     └─ FxBasketDisplay.svelte
      │                                │
      ▼                                ▼
WebSocket broadcast          connectionManager.js
                                       │
                                       ▼
                             subscriptionManager.js
                                       │
                                       ▼
                            marketDataStore.js (NEW)
                             - per-symbol stores
                             - subscription dedup
                             - latency tracking
architecture.description: Data flows from cTrader/TradingView through DataRouter to WebSocket. Frontend connectionManager dispatches to subscriptionManager which routes to component callbacks. New marketDataStore sits between subscriptionManager and components, providing single source of truth with subscription deduplication.

data_flow.diagram_ascii:
WebSocket message
       │
       ▼
validateWebSocketMessage() [DEV ONLY]
       │
       ▼
marketDataStore.handleUpdate()
       │
       ├─ calculateLatency(data)
       │
       ├─ normalizeData(data)
       │
       └─ store.update(current => ({...current, ...normalized, latency, status}))
              │
              ▼
       Svelte reactive update
              │
              ▼
       Derived stores recalculate
       - currentPrice
       - rangePercent
       - dailyChange
data_flow.description: WebSocket messages enter through validation (dev mode only), flow through marketDataStore for normalization and latency calculation, then update per-symbol stores which trigger Svelte reactive updates and derived store recalculations.

milestones.name: Create marketDataStore.js Foundation
requirements: Create per-symbol writable stores with getState() method following workspace.js pattern
Implement centralized subscription manager with reference counting and deduplication
Add latency calculation from backend timestamps (receivedAt, sentAt)
Integrate dev-mode validation from dataContracts.js
Export derived store creators (createCurrentPriceStore, createRangePercentStore, etc.)
Handle missing latency timestamps gracefully
acceptance_criteria: getMarketDataStore(symbol) returns writable store with getState() method
subscribeToSymbol() returns unsubscribe function and updates store on data
Multiple calls to subscribeToSymbol with same symbol deduplicate to single WebSocket subscription
Latency metrics calculated when timestamps present, null when missing
Dev mode validation logs appear for malformed data
All existing E2E tests pass (store not yet used by components)

normal: Subscribe to symbol receives data updates
Derived stores recalculate on base store changes
edge: Multiple subscriptions to same symbol deduplicate
Empty symbol returns store with initial state
error: Invalid WebSocket message triggers dev-mode validation warning
Connection error updates store status to 'error'

code_intents.behavior: Returns writable store for symbol, creating if not exists. Store has getState() method following workspace.js pattern. Initial state includes symbol, status='pending', all price fields null.
Subscribes to symbol via connectionManager with reference counting. Returns unsubscribe function. Updates store status to 'pending' on subscribe, 'connected' on first data, 'error' on failure.
Processes WebSocket message through processSymbolData, calculates latency from timestamps, updates store with normalized data. Dev-mode validation via dataContracts.js.
Calculates backend (sentAt - receivedAt), network (clientReceivedAt - sentAt), and e2e (clientReceivedAt - receivedAt) latency. Returns null for missing timestamps.
Returns derived store that extracts current price from symbol store. Recalculates when base store updates.
Returns derived store that calculates day range percentage using calculateDayRangePercentage from dayRangeCalculations.js.
Returns connection status from connectionManager. Replaces useDisplayState for status tracking.

milestones.name: Backend Latency Instrumentation
requirements: Add receivedAt timestamp at DataRouter entry points (routeFromCTrader, routeFromTradingView)
Add sentAt timestamp in broadcastToClients before JSON.stringify
Add schema version field (v: '1.0.0') to all messages in MessageBuilder
Timestamps are additive - frontend gracefully handles missing fields
acceptance_criteria: All WebSocket messages contain v: '1.0.0' field
All WebSocket messages contain receivedAt timestamp
All WebSocket messages contain sentAt timestamp
Existing E2E tests pass (frontend ignores new fields)
No breaking changes to message format

edge: Timestamps present in both cTrader and TradingView messages

tests.skip_reason: Backend changes verified via existing E2E tests and manual WebSocket inspection

code_intents.behavior: Add tick._receivedAt = Date.now() at method entry before buildCTraderMessage call.
Add candle._receivedAt = Date.now() at method entry before buildTradingViewMessage call.
Add message.sentAt = Date.now() before JSON.stringify. Message is mutated in place.
Add v: SCHEMA_VERSION and receivedAt: tick._receivedAt to returned message object. SCHEMA_VERSION constant = '1.0.0'.
Add v: SCHEMA_VERSION and receivedAt: candle._receivedAt to returned message object. SCHEMA_VERSION constant = '1.0.0'.

milestones.name: Migrate PriceTicker Component
requirements: Replace useWebSocketSub with marketDataStore subscription
Remove local lastData and lastMarketProfileData state
Use reactive store assignment: $: marketData = getMarketDataStore(formattedSymbol)
Preserve symbol change handling with previousSymbol pattern
Preserve flash animation behavior on price changes
All existing PriceTicker functionality maintained
acceptance_criteria: PriceTicker displays correct prices from centralized store
Symbol change triggers proper resubscription and clears stale data
Flash animations work identically to pre-migration
Market profile renders correctly from store data
Daily change percentage calculates correctly
All E2E tests involving PriceTicker pass
No console errors in development mode

normal: Ticker displays current price from store
edge: Rapid symbol changes do not cause subscription leaks

code_intents.behavior: Remove useWebSocketSub import. Add getMarketDataStore, subscribeToSymbol imports from '../stores/marketDataStore.js'.
Remove webSocketSub variable. Add unsubscribe and previousSymbol variables. Keep lastTrackedPrice for flash logic.
Add reactive block: $: marketData = getMarketDataStore(formattedSymbol); $: lastData = $marketData; This enables reactive updates when store changes.

Replace existing symbol change reactive block with store-based pattern: if (formattedSymbol !== previousSymbol && previousSymbol !== null) { unsubscribe?.(); unsubscribe = subscribeToSymbol(formattedSymbol, source); previousSymbol = formattedSymbol; }
Initialize previousSymbol = formattedSymbol. Call subscribeToSymbol and store unsubscribe. Return cleanup: () => unsubscribe?.(). Remove webSocketSub initialization.
Update to use store pattern: Clear local state, call unsubscribe?.(), resubscribe via subscribeToSymbol.

milestones.name: Migrate FloatingDisplay Component
requirements: Replace useWebSocketSub, useDisplayState, useDataCallback with marketDataStore
Remove local lastData and lastMarketProfileData state
Use reactive store assignment pattern
Preserve symbol change handling with proper cleanup
Preserve flash animation on price changes
Replace connectionStatus with getConnectionStatus()
acceptance_criteria: FloatingDisplay renders correctly from centralized store
Symbol change triggers proper resubscription
Market profile renders from store data
Connection status displays correctly
Border flash animations work identically
Price markers continue to function
All E2E tests involving FloatingDisplay pass

edge: Symbol change preserves position and size
Rapid refresh does not cause memory leaks

code_intents.behavior: Remove useWebSocketSub, useDisplayState, useDataCallback imports. Add getMarketDataStore, subscribeToSymbol, getConnectionStatus from '../stores/marketDataStore.js'.
Remove webSocketSub, displayState, handlers. Add unsubscribe, previousSymbol. Keep lastTrackedPrice for flash logic. lastData remains as reactive assignment from store.
Add: $: marketData = getMarketDataStore(formattedSymbol); $: lastData = $marketData; $: connectionStatus = getConnectionStatus(); Update reactive blocks to use store data.

Add reactive block for symbol changes with proper cleanup: if (formattedSymbol !== previousSymbol && formattedSymbol) { unsubscribe?.(); unsubscribe = subscribeToSymbol(formattedSymbol, source); lastData = null; lastMarketProfileData = null; previousSymbol = formattedSymbol; }
Initialize previousSymbol, call subscribeToSymbol with formattedSymbol and source. Remove useDataCallback.createCallback() and webSocketSub.subscribe() calls. Return cleanup calling unsubscribe?.().
Update to: lastData = null; lastMarketProfileData = null; unsubscribe?.(); unsubscribe = subscribeToSymbol(formattedSymbol, source); canvasRef?.refreshCanvas?.();

milestones.name: Migrate FxBasketDisplay and Integrate State Machine
requirements: Integrate FX Basket state machine into marketDataStore
Replace fxBasketStore, fxBasketStateMachine, fxBasketProcessor with store-based implementation
Create subscribeBasket() function that manages 30-symbol subscriptions
Create derived stores for basket values and progress
Preserve 600ms rate limiting between subscriptions
Preserve retry logic for missing pairs
acceptance_criteria: FxBasketDisplay shows all 30 currency pairs from centralized stores
State machine tracks received pairs correctly
Progress indicator shows correct count
Basket calculations are correct
Retry mechanism works for failed pairs
All E2E tests pass
No subscription leaks or memory issues

normal: All 30 pairs display after initial load
error: Error state shows after all retries fail

code_intents.behavior: Integrate fxBasketStateMachine logic into marketDataStore. Create writable store for basket state tracking (receivedPairs, expectedPairs, state, partialData). Export basketProgress derived store.

Subscribe to all 30 FX pairs with 600ms delay between each. Track subscriptions for cleanup. Update basket state machine on each data received. Return unsubscribe function that cleans up all 30 subscriptions.
Create derived store that calculates basket values (USD, EUR, GBP, etc.) from all symbol stores when basket state is 'ready'. Uses calculateAllBaskets from fxBasketCalculations.js.
Export function that resubscribes to pairs in ERROR or PENDING state. Preserves existing retry logic with MAX_AUTO_RETRIES = 2.
Remove fxBasketStore, fxBasketStateMachine, fxBasketProcessor imports. Add subscribeBasket, basketValues, basketProgress, retryMissingPairs from '../stores/marketDataStore.js'.
Remove store, stateMachine, basketData variables. Add unsubscribeBasket. Use $basketValues and $basketProgress reactive references.
Replace with: unsubscribeBasket = subscribeBasket(fxPairs); Store the returned cleanup function.
Update to: unsubscribeBasket?.(); unsubscribeBasket = subscribeBasket(fxPairs); renderCanvas();
Add unsubscribeBasket?.() call to cleanup.

milestones.name: Update dataContracts.js with Version and Latency Types
requirements: Add v (version) field to WebSocketMessage typedef
Add receivedAt and sentAt optional fields to WebSocketMessage typedef
Add LatencyMetrics typedef for latency tracking
Add validation for version field (optional for backward compatibility)
acceptance_criteria: WebSocketMessage typedef includes v, receivedAt, sentAt fields
LatencyMetrics typedef documents backend, network, e2e fields
Dev mode validation handles missing version gracefully
Existing validation continues to work

edge: Missing version does not fail validation

tests.skip_reason: Type definition changes verified via TypeScript/JSDoc tooling

code_intents.behavior: Add @property {string} [v] - Schema version (e.g., '1.0.0'). Add @property {number} [receivedAt] - Backend receive timestamp. Add @property {number} [sentAt] - WebSocket send timestamp.
Add new typedef: @typedef {Object} LatencyMetrics with @property {number} [backend], @property {number} [network], @property {number} [e2e]. Documents latency metrics structure.
Add optional version validation: if version present and not '1.0.0', add warning to result.warnings. Version is optional for backward compatibility.

waves.rationale: Foundation work (store creation, backend instrumentation, type updates) can proceed in parallel. No dependencies between these milestones.
PriceTicker migration depends on M-001 (marketDataStore). Simplest component for first migration pilot.
FloatingDisplay migration depends on M-003 success. Medium complexity with market profile integration.
FxBasketDisplay migration is most complex (30 symbols, state machine). Depends on M-004 success and all foundation work.

milestone_dependencies.diagram_ascii:
M-001 (Store) ──┬── M-003 (PriceTicker) ── M-004 (FloatingDisplay) ── M-005 (FxBasket)
                 │
M-002 (Backend) ─┤
                 │
M-006 (Types) ───┘
